mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 5.0% indicate deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns falls below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and GDP growth signal weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary pressures that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved real rate expectations.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60d returns in >70% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized increase in housing starts and retail sales forecasts indicates expanding consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The combined rise in HOUST and RSAFS fails to generate positive 20d returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes (>5.0%), declining payroll employment signals worsening labor market conditions, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In high-unemployment periods, PAYEMS declines fail to produce negative 60d returns in >60% of cases, or the regime-specific effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDP growth signals robust economic expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to generate positive 5d returns in >60% of instances, or the correlation between GDPC1 and returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply-side momentum, creating deflationary pressures that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to produce positive 60d returns in >65% of cases, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
